hypothesis: Computationally efficient EMD computed over DFT-derived band gap distributions will correlate more strongly with SUN stability scores across diverse chemical compositions than EMD computed over ML-predicted formation energies, because band gap values—being sensitive to both electronic structure and atomic geometry—capture a broader range of physically meaningful deviations from stable configurations, making band gap–based EMD a superior lightweight proxy metric for identifying chemically feasible generated structures without requiring full DFT relaxation.
